name: Pre-migration $350 quote renders as $350.00 on the public page after the cents backfill
description: The cents-shape unification (fmtMoney(cents)) caused legacy KV rows persisted as dollars to render at 1/100 their real scale on customer-facing pages. The one-shot backfill (backend/scripts/backfill-cents.ts) multiplies legacy rows × 100 and stamps _centsMigrated: true. After the script runs, every public quote/contract/invoice URL must show the original real money figure.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.role: Customer who got a fence-repair quote earlier this month
persona.context: Click-throughs from her email; sees the document her contractor sent.

preconditions: Backfill script has been run on the KV, stamping _centsMigrated:true and rewriting estimatedTotal=35000

steps.expect: Quote summary reads 'Fence repair — Patel'; estimated total renders as $350.00 (NOT $3.50)

steps.action: Looks at the line items table
steps.expect: Each line price renders cents-correct (e.g. $250.00 + $100.00, not $2.50 + $1.00)

steps.action: Action card in /assistant for the same quote
steps.expect: Already rendered $350.00 before the backfill — was sourced from totalCents in the message payload, not from the persisted DTO

evaluationCriteria.check: Public quote, public contract, public invoice all share the same cents-correct money rendering across legacy + new rows
Customer trusts the document — no $3.50 surprise that would prompt a confused reply
Backfill is idempotent (rerun is a no-op because _centsMigrated:true skips); persisted shape is consistent across stores
Money figures are readable as currency by screen readers ('three hundred fifty dollars' via the currency formatter)
